field_of_interest: Regulatory Spectrum Monitoring - Rohde & Schwarz vendor perspective
description: Supplementary corpus of publicly available Rohde & Schwarz literature describing the technology system of a regulator-grade spectrum monitoring deployment: antennas, receivers, direction finders, transportable/handheld stations, and the system software (R&S ARGUS, CEPTOR, RAMON, GSACSM). Use this WITH sources.json for a full picture, or instead of it if you specifically want to study one vendor's end-to-end view. All PDFs are hosted on rohde-schwarz.com or itu.int and were publicly available at the time of curation.
license_notice: All R&S product brochures and white papers remain copyright Rohde & Schwarz. The manifest stores URLs and metadata only; do not redistribute the PDFs without checking the R&S terms of use on each landing page.
vendor_bias_note: This corpus is by definition vendor-biased. Use it to learn the *shape* of a complete monitoring system (antenna -> receiver -> DF/locator -> software -> network) and to extract concrete technology-driver candidates, but cross-check claims against the independent sources in sources.json (ITU, CEPT, FCC, NTIA, RSPG, academic surveys).

categories.J_system_overview: R&S system-level and methodology white papers (hybrid geolocation, DF methodologies, modern monitoring vision)
categories.K_receivers: R&S monitoring receivers (ESMW, EM200, PR200) and application flyers
categories.L_direction_finders: R&S direction finders and outdoor locators (DDF550, UMS300)
categories.P_application_notes: R&S application notes on enabling techniques (real-time spectrum analysis, etc.)

sources.title: Modern Spectrum Monitoring (R&S presentation at ITU RRS-25 Africa, Session 1)
sources.why_relevant: Concise, system-level R&S view: station types (fixed, mobile, transportable, handheld, aerial), automatic measurements, cloud sharing, AI-based signal detection, and an explicit 'spectrum monitoring in the future' slide (extended frequency range, wider bandwidth, AI). Excellent driver-identification seed in one short document.

sources.title: Spectrum Monitoring with Hybrid AOA/TDOA Geolocation - White Paper
sources.why_relevant: End-to-end view of locating emitters: antennas, receivers, direction finders, compact systems and the orchestrating software. Good source for the precision-geolocation driver family.

sources.title: An Introduction to Direction Finding Methodologies - White Paper
sources.manual_reason: The previously direct CDN URL now returns 404. The whitepaper is still linked from the Direction Finding Resources landing page (see landing_page). Open it there, download the current PDF and save with the target filename in pdfs/.
sources.why_relevant: Vendor-neutral methodology primer (Doppler, Watson-Watt, correlative interferometry, TDOA) - useful baseline for the DF/geolocation driver family.

sources.title: New direction finders cope with next generation signals - Reference Card (BNetzA nationwide DDF550 modernization)
sources.why_relevant: Concrete worked example of a national regulator (BNetzA) replacing its entire VHF/UHF DF network. Useful for the 'how do regulators actually procure and roll out new monitoring infrastructure' angle.

sources.title: R&S ESMW Ultra Wideband Monitoring Receiver - Product Brochure
sources.why_relevant: State-of-the-art wideband regulator receiver: 8 kHz to 40 GHz, up to 2 GHz real-time bandwidth, 100 GbE I/Q streaming, integrated AoA DF. Direct evidence for the 'software-defined wideband receivers' driver.

sources.title: R&S EM200 Digital Compact Receiver - Product Brochure
sources.why_relevant: Compact, cost-efficient receiver for distributed deployments; shows the 'lower-cost networked sensor' trend that competes with crowdsourced/Electrosense-style approaches.

sources.title: R&S PR200 Portable Monitoring Receiver - Product Brochure
sources.why_relevant: Handheld receiver with AoA DF - demonstrates the personal/portable end of the monitoring continuum and the 'every inspector is a sensor' trend.

sources.title: 5G measurements in 3D - Product Flyer (PR200 + EM200)
sources.why_relevant: Worked example of monitoring 5G beam-steered emissions in 3D using a portable+compact receiver pair - touches the 'monitoring of beamforming/mMIMO signals' driver.

sources.title: R&S DDF550 Wideband Direction Finder - Product Brochure
sources.why_relevant: Reference high-end wideband DF used by national regulators (e.g. BNetzA). Concrete benchmark for the precision-geolocation driver.

sources.title: R&S UMS300 Compact Monitoring and Radiolocation System - Product Brochure
sources.why_relevant: Outdoor, all-in-one sensor combining monitoring + AoA + TDOA + GNSS time-sync - representative of the distributed-sensor-network driver family.

sources.title: Active Antennas - Experts for Signal Interception and Monitoring - Product Brochure
sources.why_relevant: Antenna-portfolio overview spanning HF to microwave for stationary, mobile, and tactical monitoring. Defines the 'antenna sub-system' of the technology stack and the design trade-offs (active vs. passive, omni vs. directional).

sources.title: R&S ARGUS 6.3 Spectrum Monitoring Software - Product Brochure
sources.why_relevant: The canonical regulator-grade software stack (deployed in 100+ countries). Defines the typical workflows (ITU-compliant measurements, geolocation via AoA/TDOA/hybrid, map-based ops, statistics) - i.e. the 'software systems' axis of the technology stack.

sources.title: R&S CEPTOR Software for Radiomonitoring Systems - Product Brochure
sources.why_relevant: Alternative R&S monitoring-software offering (signal collection, DF, geolocation, reporting); useful counterpoint to ARGUS for understanding the SW design space.

sources.title: R&S GSACSM Communications System Monitoring - Product Brochure
sources.why_relevant: Satellite / communications-system monitoring software with autonomous detection and classification of terrestrial+satellite signals - exemplar of the 'autonomous classifier' technology driver.

sources.title: Implementation of Real-Time Spectrum Analysis - Application Note 1EF77
sources.why_relevant: Technical primer on the enabling DSP techniques (FFT update rates, POI, frequency-mask triggering, persistence) underlying every modern monitoring receiver - useful when reasoning about the 'wideband real-time processing' driver.
